task_completion.description: Create a compact bone mask with threshold 662–1988 HU
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the InVesalius interface, look for a mask created with threshold values approximately 662 to 1988 HU visible in the threshold panel, or a mask in the list named to indicate compact bone.

task_completion.description: Create a full bone mask with threshold 226–3071 HU
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for another mask created with threshold values approximately 226 to 3071 HU visible in the threshold panel, or a mask in the list named to indicate full bone.

task_completion.description: Perform a boolean subtraction (full bone minus compact bone)
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the Boolean operations dialog being configured to subtract the compact bone mask from the full bone mask, or a third mask appearing in the list representing the cancellous bone result.

task_completion.description: Generate a 3D surface from the cancellous bone mask
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the 3D surface of the cancellous bone mask being generated and visible in the 3D viewer pane (usually bottom right).

task_completion.description: Export the 3D surface as an STL file
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for an 'Export 3D surface' dialog saving to '/home/ga/Documents/cancellous_study/cancellous_bone.stl', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file exists.
Look for a 'Save Project As' dialog saving to '/home/ga/Documents/cancellous_study/bone_analysis.inv3', or a terminal/file manager confirming the file exists.

integrity.description: The agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the operations
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show interaction with the InVesalius graphical interface for masking, boolean operations, and exporting, rather than using terminal scripts to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The boolean operation was performed using the software's built-in tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The boolean operation dialog or menu should be visible in the screenshots, showing the actual subtraction process.

integrity.description: The correct CT Cranium DICOM series is loaded
integrity.visual_evidence: The InVesalius application should display actual skull/bone structures in the 2D slice viewers, confirming the real medical imaging data is being used.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating exactly 3 masks in InVesalius 3: compact bone (662-1988 HU), full bone (226-3071 HU), and their boolean subtraction result (cancellous bone). The final STL must be exported to /home/ga/Documents/cancellous_study/cancellous_bone.stl and the project saved to /home/ga/Documents/cancellous_study/bone_analysis.inv3. The STL should contain at least 500 triangles.
